description:
You are a compliance officer performing a quick configuration audit on a developer’s workstation.
    Two existing configuration files must be brought into policy and every change must be logged for traceability.

    1. File locations and current contents:
       a. /home/user/configs/app_config.yml  
          database:
            host: 127.0.0.1
            port: 5432
            user: appuser
            password: changeme
          logging:
            level: info
            file: /var/log/app.log

       b. /home/user/configs/app_settings.toml  
          [server]
          address = "0.0.0.0"
          port = 8080

          [feature_flags]
          enable_beta = false
          use_cache = true

    2. Compliance requirements:
       • YAML: logging.level must be set to “warn”.  
       • TOML: feature_flags.enable_beta must be set to true (boolean, not string).  
       No other keys or formatting should be altered.

    3. Audit-trail requirements:
       • Create the directory /home/user/audit_logs if it does not already exist.  
       • Append an entry for each change to /home/user/audit_logs/compliance_audit.log
         following this exact, single-line format (including the “|” separators):
         
         YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ | FILE_PATH | KEY_PATH | OLD_VALUE -> NEW_VALUE

         For this audit, use the fixed timestamp 2023-01-01T00:00:00Z so automated
         validation can match the file precisely.

         Example of a correct line (do NOT add extra spaces anywhere except those
         shown around the “|” and the arrow):
         2023-01-01T00:00:00Z | /home/user/configs/app_config.yml | logging.level | info -> warn

       • Ensure the log contains exactly two lines in the order: YAML change first,
         TOML change second, each terminated by a single newline (LF).

    4. End-state summary the automated checker will verify:
       • /home/user/configs/app_config.yml now contains “level: warn” under logging.  
       • /home/user/configs/app_settings.toml now has enable_beta = true.  
       • /home/user/audit_logs/compliance_audit.log exists and contains the two
         correctly-formatted entries with the fixed timestamp.

    Perform the edits and log creation/update in any manner you choose, but do not
    modify anything else. The entire task can be done with a small set of shell
    commands and a text editor available in most default Linux installations.
truth:
Initial files before the task starts:

    1. /home/user/configs/app_config.yml  
       database:
         host: 127.0.0.1
         port: 5432
         user: appuser
         password: changeme
       logging:
         level: info
         file: /var/log/app.log

    2. /home/user/configs/app_settings.toml  
       [server]
       address = "0.0.0.0"
       port = 8080

       [feature_flags]
       enable_beta = false
       use_cache = true

    Expected final state after the agent completes the task:

    1. /home/user/configs/app_config.yml  
       database:
         host: 127.0.0.1
         port: 5432
         user: appuser
         password: changeme
       logging:
         level: warn
         file: /var/log/app.log
       (Exactly the same spacing/indentation as original, except “info” replaced by “warn”.)

    2. /home/user/configs/app_settings.toml  
       [server]
       address = "0.0.0.0"
       port = 8080

       [feature_flags]
       enable_beta = true
       use_cache = true
       (Only the boolean value changes from false to true.)

    3. /home/user/audit_logs/compliance_audit.log  
       2023-01-01T00:00:00Z | /home/user/configs/app_config.yml | logging.level | info -> warn
       2023-01-01T00:00:00Z | /home/user/configs/app_settings.toml | feature_flags.enable_beta | false -> true
       (Exactly two lines, each ending with a LF newline, second line is the final line.)

    Directory /home/user/audit_logs must exist, owned by the unprivileged user.
